title: Fabric Organizational Tags
summary: Fabric organizational tags enable multi-dimensional classification of workspaces and items. Tags are returned inline in item/workspace responses and can be used for governance, inventory, and agent-based filtering.

how_tags_work.definition: Tags are tenant-level organizational labels created by Fabric admins. Each tag has an ID (UUID), displayName, and optional scope.
how_tags_work.api_behavior: The tags array is returned on items and workspaces that have tags applied. Items/workspaces without tags omit the field entirely (same pattern as sensitivityLabel).
how_tags_work.no_server_side_filter: There is no query parameter to filter items/workspaces by tag server-side. Use JMESPath client-side filtering on the returned data.
how_tags_work.cannot_set_at_creation: Tags cannot be set during item/workspace creation. They must be applied after creation via the applyTags endpoint.

reading_tags.table_output: All list commands dynamically show a TAGS column when any item in the response has tags applied.

managing_tags.list_available_tags: fabio admin list-tags (requires Fabric Administrator role)

governance_patterns: Tag workspaces by environment: Production, Development, Staging, Sandbox
Tag items by data domain: Finance, HR, Marketing, Engineering
Tag items by data sensitivity tier to complement sensitivity labels
Scan workspaces for untagged items and flag for remediation
Use tags to implement ownership tracking (tag with team/owner identifier)
Combine tags with sensitivity labels for multi-dimensional classification

ai_agent_patterns.environment_guardrail: Check workspace tags before executing mutations — block writes to 'Production' tagged workspaces without approval
ai_agent_patterns.scope_discovery: Use tags to identify relevant workspaces/items for a task without knowing specific IDs
ai_agent_patterns.cross_workspace_scan: List all workspaces, then for each: list items and check tag coverage

prerequisites.reading_tags: Any workspace viewer role — tags are in the standard item/workspace response.
prerequisites.applying_tags: Workspace Member or higher role for item tags. Workspace Admin for workspace tags.
prerequisites.admin_crud: Fabric Administrator role for creating, updating, and deleting tag definitions.
prerequisites.rate_limit: 25 requests per minute for tag admin operations.

differences_from_sensitivity_labels.source: Tags are Fabric-native (managed by admin within Fabric). Sensitivity labels are from Microsoft Purview (external system).
differences_from_sensitivity_labels.creation_time: Sensitivity labels can be set at creation time (--sensitivity-label flag). Tags CANNOT — must apply post-creation.
differences_from_sensitivity_labels.name_resolution: Tag names (displayName) are returned inline in item responses. Sensitivity labels return only UUIDs (names require Graph API via 'fabio label list').
differences_from_sensitivity_labels.admin_requirement: Tag definition CRUD requires Fabric admin. Applying existing tags to items requires only workspace Member role.
differences_from_sensitivity_labels.bulk_operations: Sensitivity labels have dedicated bulk-set/bulk-remove admin endpoints. Tags have per-item apply/unapply only.
